title: Quit India Movement Overview
description: Comprehensive overview of the 1942 Quit India Movement led by Mahatma Gandhi, demanding an end to British rule.
author: National Archives of India

title: Partition of India (1947)
description: Historical analysis of the partition of British India into the independent nations of India and Pakistan.
author: Modern South Asia Studies

title: Non-Cooperation Movement (1920)
description: Gandhi's first major non-violent mass resistance campaign urging Indians to withdraw cooperation from British colonial rule.
author: Indian National Congress Records

title: The Salt March (Dandi March, 1930)
description: Gandhi's 240-mile march to the sea to protest the British salt tax, sparking mass civil disobedience across India.
author: Sabarmati Ashram Archive

title: Mughal Architecture Documentary
description: Visual documentary exploring Mughal architectural achievements including the Taj Mahal, Red Fort, and Fatehpur Sikri.
author: History Channel

title: Akbar's Administrative Reforms
description: In-depth study of the revenue, military and religious reforms introduced by Emperor Akbar that unified the Mughal state.
author: Mughal Studies Quarterly

title: First Battle of Panipat (1526)
description: Detailed account of Babur's decisive victory over Ibrahim Lodi that established Mughal rule in northern India.
author: Baburnama Translation Project

title: Decline of the Mughal Empire
description: Analysis of the political, economic and military factors that led to the fragmentation of the Mughal Empire in the 18th century.
author: Journal of South Asian History

title: Mauryan Empire Overview
description: Detailed overview of the Mauryan Empire under Chandragupta Maurya and Ashoka the Great, spanning most of the Indian subcontinent.
author: Ancient History Journal

title: Indus Valley Civilisation: Seals & Scripts
description: Analysis of the undeciphered Indus script and pictographic seals found at Mohenjo-daro, Harappa and Lothal.
author: Archaeological Survey of India

title: The Gupta Golden Age
description: Exploration of the remarkable advances in science, mathematics, literature and art during the Gupta period.
author: Classical India Studies

title: The Vedic Period & Early Settlements
description: Study of the early Vedic society, its religious texts, pastoral economy and the gradual settlement of the Indo-Gangetic plain.
author: Vedic Studies Institute

title: Rise of the Maratha Confederacy
description: Study of the rise and expansion of Maratha power in 17th–18th century India under Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.
author: Indian Historical Review

title: The Peshwa Era (1713–1818)
description: Political and military history of the Peshwa-led Maratha Confederacy at the height of its territorial expansion.
author: Deccan History Society

title: The Constitution of India (1950)
description: Original constitutional document of the Republic of India, adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 January 1950.
author: Constituent Assembly of India

title: Indo-Pakistan War & Bangladesh Liberation (1971)
description: Military and diplomatic history of the 1971 war resulting in the creation of Bangladesh and a decisive Indian victory.
author: South Asia Military History

title: The Green Revolution in India
description: How the introduction of high-yield crop varieties transformed Indian agriculture and ended famine in the 1960s–70s.
author: Indian Council of Agricultural Research

title: The Bhakti Movement
description: The devotional reform movement that swept medieval India, challenging caste hierarchies and orthodox religious practice.
author: Indian Philosophy Journal

title: Architecture of the Delhi Sultanate
description: Survey of the distinctive Indo-Islamic architectural styles that emerged under the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate.
author: Islamic Architecture Review

title: Aryabhata & Indian Mathematics
description: The groundbreaking contributions of Aryabhata to algebra, trigonometry, place value and astronomy in the 5th century CE.
author: Indian Science History Society
